title: centaurea savranica
intro: Centaurea savranica, also known as the Turkish cornflower or yellow knapweed, is a flowering plant in the daisy family native to the steppes and mountain meadows of Turkey, Iran, and the Caucasus. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Centaurea savranica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy in folk medicine. Centaurea savranica is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal properties, and it is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
